title: Quantized Collective Fluctuations in Correlated Fermion Systems
authors: S. S. Onuchin, Ya. S. Lyakhova, L. D. Silakov, A. N. Rubtsov
abstract: Collective excitations in fermionic systems play a crucial role in determining their physical properties. An important challenge is to develop efficient theoretical approaches for describing these excitations and their coupling to fermionic degrees of freedom. In this work, we revisit the problem of quantifying the contributions of individual bosonic modes of collective fluctuations to observable properties of correlated fermion systems within the framework of the Fluctuating Local Field (FLF) method. Whereas the auxiliary field in this method was previously considered only classically, we formulate its systematic extension termed Quantum FLF (Q-FLF) that incorporates selected bosonic Matsubara modes, thus tailoring it to description of quantum collective fluctuations. As a testbed, we apply the approach to a half-filled one-dimensional Hubbard chain and compute the Green's function, the total energy, and the antiferromagnetic susceptibility. Our results demonstrate that the proposed scheme enables an efficient and selective characterization of the contributions of individual bosonic modes. In particular, low Matsubara frequencies are found to have a quantitative impact on integrated observables such as total energy and antiferromagnetic susceptibility. At the same time, an accurate description of single-particle properties requires inclusion of higher-frequency bosonic modes.
summary: The paper introduces the Q-FLF method, an extension of the Fluctuating Local Field approach that incorporates quantum Matsubara modes to better describe collective excitations. It demonstrates that while low-frequency modes are sufficient for global thermodynamic quantities, higher-frequency modes are necessary for accurate single-particle spectra. This provides a selective tool for studying the impact of specific fluctuation channels in correlated systems.
main_problem: Developing an efficient theoretical approach to quantify the specific contributions of individual bosonic Matsubara modes (quantum fluctuations) to the observable properties of correlated fermion systems.
main_result: The proposed Q-FLF method shows that low-frequency bosonic modes quantitatively impact global observables like total energy and susceptibility, whereas an accurate description of single-particle Green's functions requires higher-frequency modes.
method: The Quantum Fluctuating Local Field (Q-FLF) method, which extends the classical FLF approach by incorporating selected bosonic Matsubara modes into a Gaussian ensemble of fluctuating external fields.
model_or_system: A one-dimensional (1D) Hubbard chain at half-filling with hopping constant t and on-site Coulomb interaction U, studied in finite sizes (N = 4, 6, 8, 10, 12).
key_observables: Matsubara Green's function, total energy per site, and antiferromagnetic susceptibility (specifically the Curie constant).
important_parameters: Interaction strength (U/t = 1), inverse temperature (beta), and system size (N).
main_assumptions: The bosonic spectrum is truncated to include only the zeroth and +/- 1 Matsubara modes; the system is modeled using a minimal approximation of small-size chains to neglect spatial inhomogeneities.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the dispersion law and Fermi level for N=4 and N=6; Figure 2 compares the Green's function for Q-FLF, FLF0, and ED; Figure 3 compares total energy per site; Figure 4 shows the temperature dependence of the Curie constant.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the Q-FLF method, applies it to the 1D Hubbard model, evaluates its performance on global and single-particle observables, provides a diagrammatic interpretation of the expansion, and compares results against exact numerical benchmarks.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. Dispersion law (solid line) and Fermi level (dashed line) of half-filled 1D Hubbard model with N = 4 (left panel) and N = 6 (right panel) sites. Occurrence of nesting between Fermi-points in N = 4 case is observed.
Figure 2. Matsubara Green’s function for Hubbard chain of N = 4 (a) and N = 6 (b) sites at β = 8 for momentum k = π in regime of moderate correlations U/t = 1.

Figure 3. Total energy per site E/N as a function of inverse temperature β for 1D half-filled Hubbard chain with N = 6 (a) and N = 8 (b) sites at U/t = 1. The comparison is between the results obtained with Q-FLF, FLF with classical mode only, and exact diagonalization.
Figure 4. Temperature dependence of Curie constant χ/β for the Hubbard chain of (a) N = 6, (b) N = 8, (c) N = 10 and (d) N = 12 in regime of moderate correlations U/t = 1 in different approximations.
